Snapper_Rocks.name: Snapper Rocks (Chinese: 史奈普岩; Indonesian/JP/KR none)
Snapper_Rocks.gps_location: Approx. -28.1640, 153.5520; Coolangatta, QLD. Google Maps: 'Snapper Rocks Headland'
Snapper_Rocks.distance_from_obx_airport_km: Gold Coast Airport (OOL) approx. 4 km / 8 min by car;Brisbane Airport (BNE) approx. 105 km / 75 min
Snapper_Rocks.wave_type: World-famous right-hand point break / Superbank head — long, fast, occasionally hollow walls running 200–600 m from Snapper to Kirra on big swells
Snapper_Rocks.skill_level: Advanced to Pro primary;Intermediate possible at smaller chest-high days but heavy localism;Beginner not recommended
Snapper_Rocks.wave_height_range: 1–10 ft (works 1 ft up to double-overhead);sweet spot 3–6 ft for clean linkup with Rainbow Bay → Greenmount
Snapper_Rocks.bottom_type: Sand (Superbank sand-bypassing system pumps sand from Tweed River, creating barrels over sandbar at the rocks)
Snapper_Rocks.best_season: Year-round but peak swell March–May (autumn cyclone season) and Sept–Nov;Quiksilver Pro Gold Coast held Mar/Apr
Snapper_Rocks.best_tide: Mid-to-high tide for cleanest sections;low tide exposes rock platform and increases hazard near takeoff
Snapper_Rocks.best_wind: SW to W (offshore);N–NE winds destroy lineup
Snapper_Rocks.best_swell_direction: SE primary (cyclone/Tasman lows);E and S also work
Snapper_Rocks.swell_consistency_rating: 5/5 — most consistent point on Gold Coast
Snapper_Rocks.wave_length_meters: Snapper section alone 100–250 m;full Superbank linkup Snapper→Rainbow→Greenmount→Kirra can run 600 m+ on rare alignments
Snapper_Rocks.wave_speed_rating: Fast — Superbank sand creates barrel sections faster than most reef points
Snapper_Rocks.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Very high — crowded, aggressive lineup of locals + visiting pros;Mick Fanning, Joel Parkinson, Stephanie Gilmore home break
Snapper_Rocks.secondary_peaks: Yes — Rainbow Bay (next bay down), Greenmount, and Kirra link on bigger swells
Snapper_Rocks.peak_crowd_level: 5/5 — one of the most crowded waves on earth in cyclone season
Snapper_Rocks.access: Park at Snapper Rocks Headland car park (limited, often full by 6am);short walk down to rock entry;or walk from Rainbow Bay sand beach
Snapper_Rocks.paddle_out_method: Jump off the rocks at the Headland between sets (timing critical);easier alternative is paddle from Rainbow Bay sand beach (longer but safer)
Snapper_Rocks.entry_fee: Free public beach;paid parking nearby AUD 2–4/hour
Snapper_Rocks.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol 5:00–7:30 am to avoid worst of crowd;sunset session can be productive but locals dominant
Snapper_Rocks.best_time_of_day: Before 7 am;weekdays significantly less crowded than weekends
Snapper_Rocks.nearby_warung: Coolangatta cafe strip 5 min walk: Hideout Coffee, Eddie's Grub House;Common Ground Coffee at Greenmount
Snapper_Rocks.alternative_spot_within_10min: Rainbow Bay (5 min walk south), Greenmount (10 min walk), D-Bah (3 min drive), Kirra (5 min drive)
Snapper_Rocks.hazards: Crowd-induced collisions are #1 hazard;sharp rocks at takeoff;localism aggression;board damage near rocks
Snapper_Rocks.marine_hazards: Bull sharks occasional (Tweed River mouth nearby);bluebottle jellyfish in summer;blue-ringed octopus in rock pools
Snapper_Rocks.reef_cut_severity_index: Low–medium (sand bottom but rocks at takeoff)
Snapper_Rocks.rip_current_pattern: Strong rip running south back through Rainbow Bay corner — useful for paddle out, watch for unwary tourists
Snapper_Rocks.lineup_etiquette: Strict hierarchy;Snapper locals (Cooly Kids) enforce priority;wait your turn, no snaking, no paddle-around
Snapper_Rocks.localism_intensity: 5/5 — among the heaviest localism in Australia;visitors expected to know the rules
Snapper_Rocks.respect_protocol: Acknowledge locals;don't paddle around;back off priority waves;don't bring 5-person tourist crews to the takeoff
Snapper_Rocks.medical_nearby: Tweed Heads Hospital 5 min drive;Coolangatta Medical Centre on Marine Pde
Snapper_Rocks.rescue_jet_ski_available: Surf Life Saving Queensland patrol Rainbow Bay daily 7 am–5 pm in season
Snapper_Rocks.nearest_surf_school: Get Wet Surf School (Coolangatta), Walkin' on Water (Currumbin) — none operate at Snapper itself due to crowd
Snapper_Rocks.board_rental_availability: Yes — Currumbin Alley Surf Hire, Snapper Rocks Boardriders shop (premium AUD 50–80/day)
Snapper_Rocks.surf_photographer_available: Yes — Surfing Australia photographers daily;@coolangatta_surfreport on IG
Snapper_Rocks.accommodation_options: Coolangatta Sands Hotel, Greenmount Beach House, Komune Resort Coolangatta;AirBnb plentiful;peak season Mar/Apr book 3+ months ahead
Snapper_Rocks.drone_allowed: Restricted near beach during patrol hours;CASA registration required for commercial
Snapper_Rocks.coworking_nearby: WOTSO Bilinga (15 min drive), Beach St Stake Hub at Burleigh (20 min)
Snapper_Rocks.avg_lesson_price: AUD 65–95 / 2-hour group lesson (none at Snapper proper;at Coolangatta or Currumbin beaches)
Snapper_Rocks.board_rental_daily: AUD 50–80 / day for soft-top;AUD 70–110 for performance shortboard / fish
Snapper_Rocks.water_quality_rating: 4/5 — generally clean;occasional Tweed River runoff after heavy rain
Snapper_Rocks.annual_competition_window: Quiksilver Pro Gold Coast (WSL CT, March/April) — when running, beach is closed to public during heats
Snapper_Rocks.community_events: Cooly Classic longboard contest (autumn);Snapper Rocks Boardriders monthly comps

Kirra.name: Kirra (Chinese: 基拉; AUS QLD)
Kirra.gps_location: Approx. -28.1697, 153.5337; Coolangatta, QLD. Google Maps: 'Kirra Surf'
Kirra.distance_from_obx_airport_km: Gold Coast Airport (OOL) approx. 2 km / 5 min by car
Kirra.wave_type: Sand-bottom right-hand point break with the world's most iconic sand barrels;south end of the Superbank;legendary on the right swell — long, perfect tubes
Kirra.skill_level: Advanced to Pro;Intermediate on smaller days but heavy hassle;Beginner not recommended
Kirra.wave_height_range: 2–8 ft;magic window 4–6 ft when sand pumps barrels
Kirra.best_season: Year-round but peak Mar–May (cyclone) and Aug–Oct;magic days require specific sand banks + SE swell
Kirra.best_swell_direction: SE primary;S and E also work
Kirra.swell_consistency_rating: 4/5 (less consistent than Snapper — needs aligned sandbanks);when on, world-class
Kirra.wave_length_meters: 150–350 m;rare alignment days 400+ m connecting from Greenmount
Kirra.wave_speed_rating: Very fast — Kirra is sand-barrel speed reference for the world
Kirra.takeoff_zone_difficulty: High — competitive lineup, fast takeoffs into immediate barrel section
Kirra.secondary_peaks: Yes — Kirra Reef (further out), Kirra Point (south side)
Kirra.peak_crowd_level: 5/5 on magic days;3/5 on average days
Kirra.access: Park at Kirra Beach car park or along Coolangatta Rd;short walk to sand
Kirra.paddle_out_method: Sand entry only — paddle out from Kirra Point in the rip corner;watch sand banks shift weekly
Kirra.nearby_warung: Kirra Beach Hotel, Kirra Sports Cafe, Bread & Butter Pastry Co.
Kirra.alternative_spot_within_10min: Snapper Rocks (5 min walk), D-Bah (3 min drive), Greenmount (5 min walk)
Kirra.hazards: Crowd snaking;sand barrel close-outs;sun exposure (no headland shade)
Kirra.marine_hazards: Bull sharks in summer (Tweed River runoff);blue-bottles
Kirra.rip_current_pattern: Strong rip on south side of Kirra Point — paddle channel
Kirra.lineup_etiquette: Strict — Kirra locals defend priority;'Save Kirra' campaign history adds local pride
Kirra.respect_protocol: Wait your turn;no party waves on barrel sets
Kirra.nearest_surf_school: None at Kirra (crowd, advanced wave);nearest at Coolangatta Beach
Kirra.board_rental_availability: Yes — Kirra Surf shop AUD 60–100/day;flagship Kirra Surf at headland
Kirra.surf_photographer_available: Yes — multiple pros work Kirra magic days
Kirra.accommodation_options: Kirra Beach Hotel, Kirra Beach Holiday Park (caravan/cabin), AirBnb
Kirra.avg_lesson_price: N/A at Kirra proper;at Coolangatta AUD 65–95
Kirra.annual_competition_window: Roxy Pro / Quiksilver Pro (when called on at Kirra);Kirra Teams Challenge invitational

Duranbah.name: Duranbah / D-Bah (Chinese: 德蘭巴; AUS NSW)
Duranbah.gps_location: Approx. -28.1707, 153.5510; Tweed Heads, NSW (just south of Snapper);Google Maps: 'Duranbah Beach'
Duranbah.distance_from_obx_airport_km: Gold Coast Airport (OOL) approx. 1 km / 3 min walk;literally next to airport runway
Duranbah.wave_type: Open beach break — multiple shifting peaks, hollow lefts and rights;less crowded than Snapper but still busy
Duranbah.skill_level: Intermediate to Advanced;some Beginner sections on smaller days
Duranbah.secondary_peaks: Yes — full length of beach has shifting peaks
Duranbah.access: Park at Duranbah Headland car park or walk from Coolangatta Beach;short walk to sand
Duranbah.paddle_out_method: Sand paddle out — pick a channel between sets;rip can help
Duranbah.nearby_warung: Coolangatta Surf Club, Eat Street Tweed (15 min drive)
Duranbah.alternative_spot_within_10min: Snapper Rocks (5 min walk), Greenmount (10 min)
Duranbah.hazards: Crowds;heavy beach break close-outs on bigger days
Duranbah.rip_current_pattern: Standard beach break rips — use for paddle out
Duranbah.localism_intensity: 3/5 — less heavy than Snapper but still respected
Duranbah.nearest_surf_school: Get Wet Surf School at Coolangatta
Duranbah.board_rental_availability: Yes — Kirra Surf, Surfer's Paradise hire shops

Burleigh_Heads.name: Burleigh Heads (Chinese: 伯利角; AUS QLD)
Burleigh_Heads.distance_from_obx_airport_km: Gold Coast Airport (OOL) approx. 18 km / 20 min;Brisbane Airport (BNE) approx. 90 km / 70 min
Burleigh_Heads.wave_type: Right-hand point break running off the headland;classic Australian point with cover-ups and long walls;'The Cove' inside section often hollow
Burleigh_Heads.skill_level: Intermediate to Advanced;sectiony at small sizes manageable for upper Intermediate;Pro on the bigger days when point connects to The Cove
Burleigh_Heads.bottom_type: Sand over rock (sand-over-rock point);headland rocks at takeoff
Burleigh_Heads.best_season: Year-round;cyclone season Feb–Apr brings biggest swells;September–November also strong
Burleigh_Heads.best_tide: Mid to higher tide;low exposes rocks at takeoff
Burleigh_Heads.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Moderate-high — locals hold priority on the headland peak
Burleigh_Heads.access: Park at Burleigh Heads car park along Goodwin Tce;walk up the point for the takeoff
Burleigh_Heads.paddle_out_method: Jump off the rocks at the headland (timing between sets);easier paddle from The Cove sand at smaller sizes
Burleigh_Heads.nearby_warung: Burleigh Pavilion, Rick Shores, Justin Lane Pizzeria, Tarte Bakery & Cafe
Burleigh_Heads.alternative_spot_within_10min: Tallebudgera Creek mouth (5 min), Palm Beach (10 min)
Burleigh_Heads.rip_current_pattern: Rip wraps around the headland — useful for paddle back
Burleigh_Heads.nearest_surf_school: Walkin' on Water at Currumbin (5 min drive), Get Wet at Coolangatta
Burleigh_Heads.accommodation_options: James Street Burleigh (boutique), Burleigh Beach Tower, AirBnb
Burleigh_Heads.annual_competition_window: Burleigh Boardriders comps;World Surf League junior events occasionally

Currumbin_Alley.name: Currumbin Alley (Chinese: 庫倫賓溪口; AUS QLD)
Currumbin_Alley.distance_from_obx_airport_km: Gold Coast Airport (OOL) approx. 5 km / 8 min
Currumbin_Alley.wave_type: Mellow right-hand point break at the mouth of Currumbin Creek;ideal for longboarders, learners, and intermediate;long peeling sections at the right size
Currumbin_Alley.skill_level: All levels — particularly strong for Beginner and Intermediate;Longboarder favorite
Currumbin_Alley.best_season: Year-round;creek mouth sand reshapes with each big swell
Currumbin_Alley.best_tide: Mid to higher tide (rising tide pushes water up creek = clean wave)
Currumbin_Alley.access: Park at Currumbin Beach car park;walk to the creek mouth
Currumbin_Alley.paddle_out_method: Easy sand paddle from inside the creek mouth
Currumbin_Alley.nearest_surf_school: Walkin' on Water Surf School (Currumbin) — premier learners' spot
Currumbin_Alley.board_rental_availability: Yes — Currumbin Alley Surf Hire AUD 30–50/day soft-top, AUD 50–80 longboard
Currumbin_Alley.nearby_warung: Pacific Pantry, Currumbin RSL, Salt Meats Cheese Burleigh
Currumbin_Alley.alternative_spot_within_10min: Palm Beach (5 min), Burleigh Heads (8 min)
Currumbin_Alley.hazards: Strong outgoing tide in creek mouth at low tide — can pull beginners out;jellyfish in summer
Currumbin_Alley.accommodation_options: Currumbin Beach Vikings Surf Club apartments, AirBnb

Greenmount_Rainbow.name: Greenmount / Rainbow Bay (Chinese: 綠山灣 / 彩虹灣; AUS QLD)
Greenmount_Rainbow.wave_type: Right-hand point break sand-and-rock combo;mid-section of the Superbank between Snapper and Kirra;long walls when sand connects
Greenmount_Rainbow.skill_level: Intermediate to Advanced;easier than Snapper proper, mellower entry
Greenmount_Rainbow.peak_crowd_level: 4/5 (less heavy than Snapper but still busy)
Greenmount_Rainbow.access: Park at Rainbow Bay car park or walk from Coolangatta;sand entry
Greenmount_Rainbow.paddle_out_method: Easy sand paddle from Rainbow Bay corner
Greenmount_Rainbow.nearest_surf_school: Get Wet Surf School (Coolangatta) operates here for learners
Greenmount_Rainbow.alternative_spot_within_10min: Snapper Rocks (5 min walk), Kirra (10 min walk)

North_Narrabeen.name: North Narrabeen (Chinese: 北納拉賓; AUS NSW)
North_Narrabeen.distance_from_obx_airport_km: Sydney Airport (SYD) approx. 32 km / 45 min;CBD 25 km / 40 min
North_Narrabeen.wave_type: Right-hand point + beach break combo;Sydney's most consistent and respected wave;Simon Anderson 3-fin invention spot
North_Narrabeen.skill_level: Intermediate to Advanced;upper beginners on smaller days at the beach section
North_Narrabeen.bottom_type: Sand with submerged rock shelves at the point
North_Narrabeen.best_wind: W to NW (offshore for the point)
North_Narrabeen.takeoff_zone_difficulty: High at the point — strict pecking order;easier at the beach
North_Narrabeen.access: Park at North Narrabeen Beach car park along Ocean St;walk to the point or the beach
North_Narrabeen.paddle_out_method: Sand entry at the beach;point lineup paddle from the rocks at the north end
North_Narrabeen.nearby_warung: Pittwater Brewing, North Narrabeen RSL, The Boathouse Palm Beach (drive)
North_Narrabeen.hazards: Crowd at the point;heavy beach close-outs on bigger swells
North_Narrabeen.marine_hazards: Bull sharks occasional in Narrabeen Lake mouth area
North_Narrabeen.localism_intensity: 4/5 — North Narrabeen Boardriders carry strong tradition
North_Narrabeen.nearest_surf_school: Manly Surf School (operates Northern Beaches);Let's Go Surfing (visits)
North_Narrabeen.accommodation_options: Narrabeen Sands Hotel, Sydney Lakeside Holiday Park (BIG4)
North_Narrabeen.annual_competition_window: Narrabeen Classic (WSL CT, March-April);Australian Open of Surfing
North_Narrabeen.community_events: North Narrabeen Boardriders monthly club comps

Manly.name: Manly Beach (Chinese: 曼利海灘; AUS NSW)
Manly.distance_from_obx_airport_km: SYD Airport approx. 25 km / 40 min by car;or Manly Ferry from Circular Quay 30 min
Manly.wave_type: Beach break with multiple peaks across the long beach — North Steyne, South Steyne, Queenscliff and Fairy Bower at the north end (rock shelf)
Manly.skill_level: All levels — Manly is Sydney's #1 learner beach (south end);Fairy Bower more advanced
Manly.bottom_type: Sand;Fairy Bower has rock shelf bottom
Manly.peak_crowd_level: 5/5 weekends and summer — tourist + lesson heavy
Manly.access: Ferry from Circular Quay (30 min) is iconic;park at Manly Wharf car park or street;walk to beach
Manly.nearest_surf_school: Manly Surf School (longest-running in Australia, est. 1985);Let's Go Surfing also operates
Manly.board_rental_availability: Yes — multiple kiosks along the Corso;Manly Surfboards AUD 30–50 soft-top, AUD 50–80 hard board
Manly.nearby_warung: The Pantry, Hugos Manly, 4 Pines Brewing Co, Bear Cottage Cafe
Manly.alternative_spot_within_10min: Freshwater (5 min), Queenscliff (walk), Curl Curl (10 min)
Manly.hazards: Bluebottle jellyfish in summer (warm currents);bull sharks occasional;crowd
Manly.rip_current_pattern: Standard beach rips;flags mark patrol zones (between flags = safest)
Manly.localism_intensity: 2/5 — Manly is the friendliest mainstream beach in Sydney
Manly.accommodation_options: Manly Pacific Hotel, Q Station Hotel (heritage), AirBnb plentiful
Manly.annual_competition_window: Australian Open of Surfing (Feb);Manly Boardriders comps

Maroubra.name: Maroubra Beach (Chinese: 馬魯巴拉; AUS NSW)
Maroubra.wave_type: Heavy powerful beach break;south-facing exposed beach catches biggest swells in Sydney;hollow lefts and rights, often closing out on size
Maroubra.skill_level: Intermediate to Advanced;the Bra Boys home break carries reputation
Maroubra.access: Park along Marine Pde or Maroubra Beach car park;walk to beach
Maroubra.paddle_out_method: Sand entry;use beach rips to paddle out efficiently
Maroubra.nearest_surf_school: Let's Go Surfing operates here;Maroubra Surf School
Maroubra.hazards: Heavy beach break close-outs;crowd at the famed 'Bra' peak
Maroubra.localism_intensity: 4/5 — Bra Boys legacy still felt;respect locals
Maroubra.accommodation_options: Maroubra Coogee AirBnb;limited hotel options nearby
Maroubra.annual_competition_window: Maroubra Boardriders comps;Bra Boys legacy events

Bondi.name: Bondi Beach (Chinese: 邦迪海灘; AUS NSW)
Bondi.wave_type: Iconic urban beach break;multiple peaks, north end (Ben Buckler) often best;south end is the famous tourist promenade
Bondi.skill_level: All levels — heavily lesson-dominated south end;north end Intermediate+
Bondi.swell_consistency_rating: 4/5 — less consistent than Manly or Maroubra
Bondi.peak_crowd_level: 5/5 — most crowded learner beach in Sydney
Bondi.access: Bus or drive to Campbell Pde;limited paid parking;walk to beach
Bondi.nearest_surf_school: Let's Go Surfing (HQ at Bondi);Bondi Surf Co
Bondi.nearby_warung: Iconic Bondi cafe scene — Bills, Speedos, Brown Sugar, Trio
Bondi.alternative_spot_within_10min: Tamarama (5 min, locals-only), Bronte (10 min walk)
Bondi.hazards: Crowd;tourist hazards;rip currents (famously documented in Bondi Rescue TV show)
Bondi.rip_current_pattern: Famous Backpacker's Express rip near south end
Bondi.accommodation_options: Bondi Beach Astra, AirBnb, hostels;peak summer expensive

Byron_Bay.name: The Pass / Wategos (Byron Bay) (Chinese: 拜倫灣; AUS NSW)
Byron_Bay.wave_type: Long mellow right-hand point break wrapping around the Cape Byron headland;classic longboarder paradise;The Pass is the iconic peeler
Byron_Bay.skill_level: All levels — premier learner-to-intermediate point break in Australia
Byron_Bay.bottom_type: Sand over rocks at the point
Byron_Bay.best_tide: Mid to high tide for cleanest peelers
Byron_Bay.peak_crowd_level: 5/5 — most crowded longboard break in Australia in peak season
Byron_Bay.access: Park at Captain Cook Lookout car park (fills by 7 am) or walk from Clarkes Beach;short walk to The Pass
Byron_Bay.paddle_out_method: Sand entry from The Pass beach;easy paddle
Byron_Bay.nearest_surf_school: Let's Go Surfing Byron Bay, Soul Surf School, Style Surfing School
Byron_Bay.board_rental_availability: Yes — multiple shops AUD 30–60 soft-top, AUD 50–90 longboard
Byron_Bay.nearby_warung: Three Blue Ducks, Bayleaf Cafe, The Top Shop Bakery
Byron_Bay.alternative_spot_within_10min: Watego's Beach (next bay, more advanced), Cosy Corner, Tallow Beach (5 min)
Byron_Bay.hazards: Crowd — extreme during peak season;mid-tide rocks near takeoff
Byron_Bay.accommodation_options: Byron at Byron Resort, Atlantic Byron Bay, AirBnb plentiful;peak summer book 6+ months ahead

Lennox_Head.name: Lennox Head (Chinese: 萊諾克斯角; AUS NSW)
Lennox_Head.wave_type: Long world-class right-hand point break wrapping around Lennox Headland;considered one of Australia's best points;500 m+ rides on the right swell
Lennox_Head.access: Park at Lennox Headland car park;walk down the rocks to entry
Lennox_Head.paddle_out_method: Jump off the boulders at the headland (timing critical) or paddle from Boulder Beach (longer)
Lennox_Head.nearest_surf_school: Lennox Head Surf School;Soul Surf School Byron
Lennox_Head.nearby_warung: Lennox Wholefoods, Quattro Café, Foam Espresso
Lennox_Head.alternative_spot_within_10min: Boulder Beach (next bay, sand), Pat Morton Lookout (point), Sharpes Beach
Lennox_Head.hazards: Boulders at entry/exit;crowd;heavy waves on bigger days;sharks (history)
Lennox_Head.marine_hazards: Great White sightings (Northern NSW has tagged populations);bull sharks
Lennox_Head.accommodation_options: Lennox Beach House Holiday Park, AirBnb, motels in town

Crescent_Head.name: Crescent Head (Chinese: 新月角; AUS NSW)
Crescent_Head.wave_type: Classic long right-hand point break;Australia's first declared National Surfing Reserve (2003);longboard mecca with mellow walls
Crescent_Head.skill_level: Intermediate;upper Beginner OK on smaller days
Crescent_Head.access: Park along Pacific St near Crescent Head SLSC;walk to the point
Crescent_Head.paddle_out_method: Sand entry from the beach corner;easy paddle
Crescent_Head.nearest_surf_school: Cresco Surf School;Mid North Coast Surf School
Crescent_Head.nearby_warung: Crescent Head Tavern, Surfing Sunday Cafe
Crescent_Head.hazards: Reef sections at low tide;crowd in school holidays

Merewether.name: Merewether Beach (Newcastle) (Chinese: 紐卡素 麥瑞威瑟; AUS NSW)
Merewether.wave_type: Powerful beach break with rock outcrops creating wedging peaks;Mark Richards (4× world champ) home break;Sandshoes outer reef on bigger days
Merewether.access: Park along Watkins St or Henderson Pde;walk to beach
Merewether.paddle_out_method: Sand paddle;use rips on bigger days
Merewether.board_rental_availability: Yes — Pacific Dreams Surfboards Merewether (legendary local shop)
Merewether.alternative_spot_within_10min: Newcastle Beach (10 min), Bar Beach (5 min)
Merewether.hazards: Heavy beach break close-outs;rocks on inside
Merewether.annual_competition_window: Surfest (oldest WSL pro-am event in southern hemisphere) — Feb/March

Bells_Beach.name: Bells Beach (Chinese: 貝爾斯海灘; AUS VIC)
Bells_Beach.distance_from_obx_airport_km: Melbourne (MEL) airport approx. 115 km / 1h 30 min;Avalon (AVV) approx. 50 km / 50 min
Bells_Beach.wave_type: World-heritage right-hand point/reef break;long powerful walls;home of the Rip Curl Pro since 1962 — longest-running surf event in the world
Bells_Beach.skill_level: Intermediate to Advanced;upper Beginner not recommended at proper Bells
Bells_Beach.wave_height_range: 3–15 ft;Bells truly comes alive at 6–10 ft when 'The Bells Bowl' sections light up
Bells_Beach.best_season: Autumn–spring (Mar–Oct);peak April for the Rip Curl Pro
Bells_Beach.swell_consistency_rating: 5/5 in season — cold Southern Ocean reliability
Bells_Beach.wave_length_meters: 200–500 m on connecting Bells Bowl days
Bells_Beach.takeoff_zone_difficulty: High at the proper Bells Bowl;easier at Rincon (adjacent peak)
Bells_Beach.access: Park at Bells Beach lookout car park;walk down the long staircase to the beach (200+ steps)
Bells_Beach.paddle_out_method: Sand entry from beach;long paddle out into the lineup
Bells_Beach.nearby_warung: Torquay (10 min drive) — Salty Dog Cafe, Bottle of Milk, Vue Cafe
Bells_Beach.alternative_spot_within_10min: Winkipop (right next door — see separate entry), Southside, Rincon, Centreside
Bells_Beach.hazards: Cold water (Southern Ocean — 4mm wetsuit standard);powerful waves;great white sharks present (Victoria/SA shark coast);crowded Pro period
Bells_Beach.marine_hazards: Great white sharks (Otway shark zone);southern right whales migrating May–Oct
Bells_Beach.localism_intensity: 3/5 — generally respectful, but Pro-period crowd extreme
Bells_Beach.nearest_surf_school: Torquay Surf Academy (in Torquay, not at Bells — too advanced for lessons)
Bells_Beach.board_rental_availability: Yes — Rip Curl flagship store Torquay AUD 50–100/day
Bells_Beach.avg_lesson_price: AUD 65–95 / 2-hour group (at Torquay beach, not Bells)
Bells_Beach.accommodation_options: Quest Torquay, Anglesea / Lorne accommodation, AirBnb;Easter book 1+ year ahead
Bells_Beach.annual_competition_window: Rip Curl Pro Bells Beach (WSL CT, Easter, April — typically Mar 30–Apr 10)

Winkipop.name: Winkipop (Chinese: 溫基波; AUS VIC)
Winkipop.gps_location: Approx. -38.3690, 144.2860; just east of Bells Beach, Torquay
Winkipop.wave_type: Right-hand reef break — Bells' faster, more demanding neighbor;four distinct sections (Uppers, Lowers, Sausage, Bowl);considered faster and more critical than Bells
Winkipop.skill_level: Advanced to Pro;less forgiving than Bells
Winkipop.access: Same as Bells — park at Bells lookout, walk down staircase, paddle slightly south
Winkipop.paddle_out_method: Sand entry then paddle around;rip channel between Winki and Bells helps
Winkipop.hazards: Same as Bells — cold, powerful, sharks possible
Winkipop.annual_competition_window: Sometimes contested as Rip Curl Pro venue when Bells inconsistent

Phillip_Island.name: Phillip Island Cape Woolamai (Chinese: 菲利普島; AUS VIC)
Phillip_Island.gps_location: Approx. -38.5483, 145.3500; Cape Woolamai, Phillip Island, Victoria
Phillip_Island.wave_type: Open beach break beneath the granite cliffs of Cape Woolamai;multiple peaks;Victoria's most exposed mainland beach
Phillip_Island.access: 1.5–2 hr drive from Melbourne;park at Cape Woolamai SLSC;walk down to beach
Phillip_Island.alternative_spot_within_10min: Smiths Beach (sand, mellower), Right Point, Express Point (Phillip Island)
Phillip_Island.hazards: Cold water;powerful surf;rip currents;great whites Bass Strait
Phillip_Island.accommodation_options: Phillip Island Caravan Park, Cowes hotels, AirBnb;famous Penguin Parade nearby

Margaret_River_Main.name: Margaret River Main Break (Chinese: 瑪格麗特河主浪點; AUS WA)
Margaret_River_Main.distance_from_obx_airport_km: Perth (PER) airport approx. 280 km / 3h 30 min;Busselton-Margaret River Airport (BQB) approx. 45 km
Margaret_River_Main.wave_type: Big-wave reef break (paddle, no tow needed mostly);powerful long lefts and rights from an outer reef;Margaret River Pro venue
Margaret_River_Main.wave_height_range: 4–20 ft;sweet spot 6–10 ft;goes XXL on a serious south swell
Margaret_River_Main.best_season: Year-round but peak Mar–May (autumn) and Sep–Nov;winter (Jun–Aug) brings biggest XXL swells
Margaret_River_Main.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Very high — deep paddle out, long swims to lineup
Margaret_River_Main.access: Park at Prevelly car park;moderate walk down to the beach;jump in and paddle the channel out
Margaret_River_Main.paddle_out_method: Use the deep channel at the south end of Surfers Point — long paddle but only safe option
Margaret_River_Main.nearest_surf_school: Yallingup Surf School (35 min north);Margaret River Surf School in town
Margaret_River_Main.board_rental_availability: Yes — Wave Wreckers, Margaret River Surf Co
Margaret_River_Main.nearby_warung: Margaret River cafe scene 10 min drive;Sea Gardens Beach Cafe at Prevelly
Margaret_River_Main.alternative_spot_within_10min: Surfers Point (next reef), The Box (hollower section), Gracetown (15 min)
Margaret_River_Main.hazards: Great White sharks (WA has highest GW attack rate);long paddle out;cold water (Indian Ocean S);swift currents
Margaret_River_Main.marine_hazards: Great White Sharks — multiple fatal incidents in WA Margaret River region history
Margaret_River_Main.accommodation_options: Margaret River Hotel, Prevelly Beach House, AirBnb wine-country options
Margaret_River_Main.annual_competition_window: Margaret River Pro (WSL CT, April–May);Drug Aware Pro

North_Point_WA.name: North Point (Gracetown WA) (Chinese: 北角; AUS WA)
North_Point_WA.wave_type: Right-hand reef-break slab;one of the most perfect, heavy right-hand barrels in Australia;NOT a learner wave
North_Point_WA.skill_level: Advanced to Pro only — slab paddle wave
North_Point_WA.access: Park at Cowaramup Bay car park Gracetown;walk along the coast or paddle from the bay
North_Point_WA.paddle_out_method: Long paddle from Gracetown beach;use channel
North_Point_WA.hazards: Shallow reef;great whites;wave power 1.5x Margaret River Main
North_Point_WA.marine_hazards: Great Whites — multiple attack history in Gracetown area
North_Point_WA.accommodation_options: Gracetown caravan park, Margaret River hotels (15 min south)

Cactus_Beach.name: Cactus Beach (Chinese: 仙人掌海灘; AUS SA)
Cactus_Beach.wave_type: Remote desert reef breaks — three main spots (Castles, Caves, Cunns) offering lefts and rights;Australia's iconic remote surf trip destination
Cactus_Beach.access: 21 km dirt road from Penong (Eyre Highway, 7 hr drive from Adelaide);4WD recommended;bush camping
Cactus_Beach.paddle_out_method: Paddle from beach;deep channels at each reef
Cactus_Beach.nearby_warung: Penong bakery 21 km away;bring all supplies
Cactus_Beach.hazards: Great White Sharks — Cactus is the original 'sharkiest' spot in Australia legend;remote rescue access;sun exposure;snakes
Cactus_Beach.marine_hazards: Great Whites — multiple sightings, one fatality (Mark Penkalla 1991)
Cactus_Beach.accommodation_options: Bush camping at Cactus (small fee), Penong Hotel, swag camping common

The_Right_WA.name: The Right (Chinese: 右浪; AUS WA)
The_Right_WA.gps_location: Approx. -34.9; 116.0 (Walpole area); precise location closely guarded
The_Right_WA.wave_type: World's heaviest right-hand slab — Russell Bierke / Mark Mathews territory;tow-only or paddle on very specific conditions;thick lurching wedge over shallow reef
The_Right_WA.access: Boat-only access from Walpole or Albany;closed location, locally protected
The_Right_WA.hazards: Extreme — wave thickness 30+ ft, can hold riders down 2+ waves;rescue distant
The_Right_WA.annual_competition_window: Red Bull Cape Fear (when run);invitationals only

Shipsterns_Bluff.name: Shipstern's Bluff (Chinese: 船尾岩; AUS TAS)
Shipsterns_Bluff.gps_location: Approx. -43.2050, 147.7980; Cape Raoul, Tasman Peninsula, Tasmania
Shipsterns_Bluff.wave_type: Heaviest slab wave in Tasmania, arguably world;'staircase' wave with multiple steps in the face;Andy Irons / Tyler Wright / Russell Bierke charging history
Shipsterns_Bluff.access: Boat from Hobart or hike 2 hr from Cape Raoul track trailhead;jet-ski tow standard
Shipsterns_Bluff.hazards: Cold water (Bass Strait/Southern Ocean) — 4mm wetsuit + hood;step-shaped wave;remote rescue;great whites
Shipsterns_Bluff.annual_competition_window: Red Bull Cape Fear (occasionally held here);invitationals

Cape_Fear.name: Cape Fear / Cape Solander (Chinese: 索蘭德角; AUS NSW)
Cape_Fear.wave_type: Right-hand slab below the cliffs at Cape Solander, Botany Bay National Park;hosting Red Bull Cape Fear pro invitational
Cape_Fear.access: Kurnell National Park (entry fee), 20 min drive from Cronulla;walk to cliff edge
Cape_Fear.paddle_out_method: Tow-in from jet-ski (closest channel inaccessible);extreme paddle possible on smallest days
Cape_Fear.hazards: Cliff base wave;rescue extremely difficult;great whites
Cape_Fear.annual_competition_window: Red Bull Cape Fear pro invitational (March/April)

Yallingup.name: Yallingup (Chinese: 雅靈格普; AUS WA)
Yallingup.wave_type: Right-hand reef break;family-friendly Margaret River region wave;mellower than Margies Main
Yallingup.skill_level: All levels — Beginner OK on smaller days
Yallingup.access: Park at Yallingup Beach car park;easy beach access
Yallingup.nearby_warung: Yallingup General Store, Caves House Hotel restaurant
Yallingup.hazards: Great whites WA;reef sections at low tide
Yallingup.accommodation_options: Caves House Hotel (heritage), AirBnb, Smiths Beach Resort

Sandon_Point.name: Sandon Point (Wollongong) (Chinese: 桑頓角; AUS NSW)
Sandon_Point.wave_type: Right-hand point/reef break;Wollongong region classic;longboard friendly to performance on bigger days
Sandon_Point.access: Park along Sandon Point Headland Reserve;walk to rocks
Sandon_Point.paddle_out_method: Jump off rocks at the headland or paddle from sand

Gnaraloo_Tombstones.name: Gnaraloo Tombstones (Chinese: 諾洛 墓碑; AUS WA)
Gnaraloo_Tombstones.wave_type: Heavy left-hand reef-pass slab;Indo-quality barrels in remote WA;Tombstones, Centres, The Bommie nearby
Gnaraloo_Tombstones.access: 1,200 km drive north of Perth (12+ hr);Gnaraloo Station gate fee;4WD recommended for last 80 km
Gnaraloo_Tombstones.nearby_warung: Gnaraloo Station camp kitchen;bring own supplies
Gnaraloo_Tombstones.hazards: Remote — limited rescue;sharks (tiger sharks present);coral cuts;sun exposure
Gnaraloo_Tombstones.accommodation_options: Gnaraloo Station Homestead or Bay Camp;camping with own setup

Bombo_Kiama.name: Bombo Beach (Kiama) (Chinese: 邦波 基亞馬; AUS NSW)
Bombo_Kiama.wave_type: Powerful beach break south of Wollongong;long stretch of sand with multiple peaks;famous Kiama Blowhole nearby
Bombo_Kiama.access: Park at Bombo Beach car park
Bombo_Kiama.alternative_spot_within_10min: Kiama Surf Beach, Cathedral Rocks, Werri Beach (15 min)

Surf_Schools_Australia.name: Surf Schools — Australia (Chinese: 衝浪學校 / AUS)
Surf_Schools_Australia.wave_type: ASI (Academy of Surfing Instructors) accreditation governs Australian surf schools;safety standards highest globally
Surf_Schools_Australia.skill_level: All levels — most schools cater B–I
Surf_Schools_Australia.wave_height_range: Most lessons run at chest-high or less for safety
Surf_Schools_Australia.best_season: Year-round per region (Gold Coast year-round; NSW Oct–Apr peak;Vic Nov–Mar warm)
Surf_Schools_Australia.access: School meets at beach pavilion or shop;board/wetsuit provided
Surf_Schools_Australia.nearest_surf_school: Top schools: Get Wet (Gold Coast), Manly Surf School (Sydney est. 1985), Let's Go Surfing (multiple), Walkin' on Water (Currumbin), Soul Surf School (Byron), Rip Curl School of Surf (Torquay)
Surf_Schools_Australia.accommodation_options: Surf-stay camps available — Surfing Mums Camp Byron, Camp Treetops Byron, Surf School Margaret River
Surf_Schools_Australia.entry_fee: Public beach access free;school fees apply

Board_Rental_Australia.name: Board Rental / Repair — Australia (Chinese: 衝浪板出租維修 / AUS)
Board_Rental_Australia.gps_location: Major surf towns: Gold Coast (Kirra Surf, Snapper Boardriders), Sydney (Sydney Surf Hire, Manly Surfboards), Byron Bay, Torquay (Rip Curl flagship), Margaret River (Wave Wreckers)
Board_Rental_Australia.wave_type: Full quiver options: soft-tops for learners through HPSB, fish, longboard, step-up for experienced
Board_Rental_Australia.board_rental_availability: Soft-top AUD 25–60/day;mid-length / longboard AUD 50–90/day;HPSB / fish AUD 60–120/day;multi-day discounts common
Board_Rental_Australia.nearest_surf_school: Most rental shops also run lessons
Board_Rental_Australia.avg_lesson_price: Bundle deals available — lesson + week rental AUD 200–350
Board_Rental_Australia.access: Drop-in walk-up;some shops offer beach delivery service for premium

Surf_Forecast_Australia.name: Surf Forecasts / Tools — Australia (Chinese: 浪況預報 / AUS)
Surf_Forecast_Australia.wave_type: Primary tools: Swellnet (free + premium, Australian-built), Surfline (global, premium for HD cams + 16-day), Coastalwatch (heritage Aussie forecaster, free), Magicseaweed (legacy, now part of Surfline), BOM Marine Wind (free official weather bureau)
Surf_Forecast_Australia.swell_consistency_rating: Each tool has strengths — Swellnet best AU east coast nuance;Surfline best HD cams;Coastalwatch best free option;BOM for official wind
Surf_Forecast_Australia.best_swell_direction: Cross-reference 2+ tools for major sessions;buoy data (Cape du Couedic, Sydney Norah Head) for ground truth

Visa_Entry_AU.name: Visa & Entry — Australia (Chinese: 簽證入境 / AUS)
Visa_Entry_AU.wave_type: Working Holiday Visa (subclass 417) for age 18–35 nationals of approved countries (Taiwan eligible since 2004);eVisitor (subclass 651) for EU citizens free;Electronic Travel Authority (ETA, subclass 601) for Taiwan/Japan/Korea/HK/Singapore AUD 20
Visa_Entry_AU.best_season: Apply 1–2 months ahead;peak summer Dec–Feb processing slower
Visa_Entry_AU.access: All applications online via Home Affairs ImmiAccount
Visa_Entry_AU.entry_fee: ETA AUD 20;eVisitor free;Working Holiday AUD 635;Student Visa AUD 710+
Visa_Entry_AU.hazards: Customs strict — declare food/wood/soil;biosecurity laws enforced;surfboards must be clean (no marine growth/seaweed) — declare them
Visa_Entry_AU.accommodation_options: Address required on arrival card;BackPacker hostels common for WHV arrivals

Quiksilver_Pro_Bells_Festivals.name: Pro Events Calendar — Australia (Chinese: 職業賽事 / AUS)
Quiksilver_Pro_Bells_Festivals.wave_type: WSL Championship Tour annual: Quiksilver Pro Snapper (Mar/Apr), Rip Curl Pro Bells Beach (Easter, Apr), Margaret River Pro (Apr–May), Narrabeen Classic (Mar);Surfest Newcastle (oldest pro-am, Feb);Australian Open of Surfing Manly (Feb);Red Bull Cape Fear (slab invitational, Mar);Vissla Sydney Surf Pro
Quiksilver_Pro_Bells_Festivals.best_season: WSL CT events Mar–May (Australian leg);state titles winter
Quiksilver_Pro_Bells_Festivals.peak_crowd_level: 5/5 during event weeks at host beaches
Quiksilver_Pro_Bells_Festivals.access: Free public beach access — book accommodation 6+ months ahead for Bells Easter and Snapper March
Quiksilver_Pro_Bells_Festivals.accommodation_options: Premium pricing during event windows — Easter Bells 3x normal rates

Avalon_Beach.distance_from_obx_airport_km: Sydney Airport (SYD) approx. 40 km / 50 min;parking on Old Barrenjoey Road
Avalon_Beach.best_wind: W / SW offshore;NE morning glass possible
Avalon_Beach.best_swell_direction: E / SE groundswell 14s+;works on S in northern bays
Avalon_Beach.paddle_out_method: Beach paddle-out;watch rip and use channel where present
Avalon_Beach.nearby_warung: Local cafes & surf shops within walking distance
Avalon_Beach.alternative_spot_within_10min: Newport, Bilgola, Whale Beach (all within 10 min north/south)
Avalon_Beach.hazards: Rip currents (notably north end);occasional shark sightings;summer bluebottle jellyfish
Avalon_Beach.marine_hazards: Bluebottles seasonal;rare shark encounters; sea urchins on reef sections
Avalon_Beach.rip_current_pattern: Channels at each end of bay; flash rips after sets
Avalon_Beach.lineup_etiquette: Australian standard: priority to deepest paddler;respect locals
Avalon_Beach.respect_protocol: Wait your turn, don't drop in, no aggressive paddle-battles
Avalon_Beach.medical_nearby: Standard ambulance dial 000;nearest hospital within 10–30 min
Avalon_Beach.rescue_jet_ski_available: Surf Life Saving patrol in season (Sep–Apr) on patrolled beaches
Avalon_Beach.nearest_surf_school: Avalon Surf Centre, Manly Surf School (extension)
Avalon_Beach.accommodation_options: Holiday rentals, caravan parks, hotels nearby
Avalon_Beach.drone_allowed: Yes outside no-fly zones; check CASA app
Avalon_Beach.annual_competition_window: Local boardrider clubs run year-round comps
Avalon_Beach.name: Avalon Beach (Chinese: 阿瓦隆海灘;not AU-pop common in CN)
Avalon_Beach.gps_location: Approx. -33.6360, 151.3290; Avalon, NSW. Google Maps: 'Avalon Beach'
Avalon_Beach.wave_type: Sand-bottom beach break with multiple peaks across 800m beach;long left & right walls on E groundswell;quality A-frame on E/NE
Avalon_Beach.skill_level: Intermediate primary; Beginner manageable on smaller days; Advanced on solid swells
Avalon_Beach.best_season: Mar–Aug (autumn / winter East-Coast lows);also Apr–May tropical pulses

13th_Beach.wave_type: Long sand-bottom beach break with multiple peaks across 6km;quality A-frames on SW swell;Australian Open Surfing host venue
13th_Beach.skill_level: Intermediate to Advanced;Beginner not recommended (no patrols on most sections)

North_Stradbroke_Point_Lookout.distance_from_obx_airport_km: Brisbane Airport (BNE) approx. 75 km + 30-min Cleveland-NSI ferry;Gold Coast Airport (OOL) 110 km + ferry
North_Stradbroke_Point_Lookout.paddle_out_method: Beach paddle-out;watch rip and use channel where present
North_Stradbroke_Point_Lookout.nearby_warung: Local cafes & surf shops within walking distance
North_Stradbroke_Point_Lookout.hazards: Rocks & reef on points;sharks (Great White & Bull);ferry-only access (last ferry 18:00)
North_Stradbroke_Point_Lookout.marine_hazards: Confirmed Great White & Bull Shark sightings — local lore high
North_Stradbroke_Point_Lookout.rip_current_pattern: Channels at each end of bay; flash rips after sets
North_Stradbroke_Point_Lookout.lineup_etiquette: Australian standard: priority to deepest paddler;respect locals
North_Stradbroke_Point_Lookout.respect_protocol: Wait your turn, don't drop in, no aggressive paddle-battles
North_Stradbroke_Point_Lookout.medical_nearby: Standard ambulance dial 000;nearest hospital within 10–30 min
North_Stradbroke_Point_Lookout.rescue_jet_ski_available: Surf Life Saving patrol in season (Sep–Apr) on patrolled beaches
North_Stradbroke_Point_Lookout.accommodation_options: Holiday rentals, caravan parks, hotels nearby
North_Stradbroke_Point_Lookout.drone_allowed: Yes outside no-fly zones; check CASA app
North_Stradbroke_Point_Lookout.annual_competition_window: Local boardrider clubs run year-round comps
North_Stradbroke_Point_Lookout.name: North Stradbroke — Point Lookout (Chinese: 北斯特拉布魯克島 · 海角望點;Quandamooka)
North_Stradbroke_Point_Lookout.gps_location: Approx. -27.4290, 153.5400; Point Lookout, NSI (Minjerribah), QLD. Google Maps: 'Point Lookout Stradbroke'
North_Stradbroke_Point_Lookout.wave_type: Cluster of reef + point breaks (Cylinder Beach, Main Beach, Frenchman's Beach);right-hand reef points + sand beaches;world-class on SE cyclone swells
North_Stradbroke_Point_Lookout.skill_level: 全程度 — Beginner at Cylinder, Intermediate-Advanced at reef points

Red_Bluff_WA.distance_from_obx_airport_km: Carnarvon Airport (CVQ) approx. 130 km gravel road / 2 hr;Perth Airport (PER) approx. 1100 km / 11 hr (or fly to CVQ)
Red_Bluff_WA.peak_crowd_level: 1/5 平日;2/5 週末 (remote location keeps crowds away)
Red_Bluff_WA.access: 4WD only (gravel road from Carnarvon);Red Bluff Campground bookable through Quobba Station Stay
Red_Bluff_WA.paddle_out_method: Off the reef shelf at low tide;timing crucial
Red_Bluff_WA.entry_fee: Quobba Station Stay daily fee: AUD $20–30 per person;camping AUD $40/site
Red_Bluff_WA.nearby_warung: Local cafes & surf shops within walking distance
Red_Bluff_WA.hazards: Sharp coral reef;remote location (130 km from nearest town);shark territory (Great White confirmed);strong currents
Red_Bluff_WA.marine_hazards: Great White Sharks confirmed;Tiger Sharks seasonal
Red_Bluff_WA.rip_current_pattern: Channels at each end of bay; flash rips after sets
Red_Bluff_WA.lineup_etiquette: Australian standard: priority to deepest paddler;respect locals
Red_Bluff_WA.respect_protocol: Wait your turn, don't drop in, no aggressive paddle-battles
Red_Bluff_WA.medical_nearby: Carnarvon Hospital 130 km / 2 hr drive;Royal Flying Doctor Service for emergencies
Red_Bluff_WA.rescue_jet_ski_available: No — remote, no SLSA patrol; bring full medical kit
Red_Bluff_WA.accommodation_options: Quobba Station Stay (camping + safari tents);bring food + water + fuel
Red_Bluff_WA.drone_allowed: Yes outside no-fly zones; check CASA app
Red_Bluff_WA.annual_competition_window: Local boardrider clubs run year-round comps
Red_Bluff_WA.name: Red Bluff (Chinese: 紅崖;WA Coral Coast — remote Quobba Station)
Red_Bluff_WA.gps_location: Approx. -24.0270, 113.4100; Quobba Station, north of Carnarvon, WA. Google Maps: 'Red Bluff Quobba'
Red_Bluff_WA.wave_type: World-class left-hand reef point break — fast, hollow walls running over reef + sand bottom;known as 'Mexican Pipeline of WA'
Red_Bluff_WA.skill_level: Advanced primary;Intermediate on smaller days; Beginner not appropriate (remote, no rescue)

Cape_Naturaliste_Bunker_Bay.distance_from_obx_airport_km: Busselton Airport approx. 30 km / 30 min;Perth Airport approx. 270 km / 3 hr
Cape_Naturaliste_Bunker_Bay.best_swell_direction: SW wraps around cape;works on W as well
Cape_Naturaliste_Bunker_Bay.paddle_out_method: Beach paddle-out;watch rip and use channel where present
Cape_Naturaliste_Bunker_Bay.nearby_warung: Local cafes & surf shops within walking distance
Cape_Naturaliste_Bunker_Bay.hazards: Sharks (Great White confirmed — fatal attack 2010);rocks at point;rips on outer sandbanks
Cape_Naturaliste_Bunker_Bay.rip_current_pattern: Channels at each end of bay; flash rips after sets
Cape_Naturaliste_Bunker_Bay.lineup_etiquette: Australian standard: priority to deepest paddler;respect locals
Cape_Naturaliste_Bunker_Bay.respect_protocol: Wait your turn, don't drop in, no aggressive paddle-battles
Cape_Naturaliste_Bunker_Bay.medical_nearby: Standard ambulance dial 000;nearest hospital within 10–30 min
Cape_Naturaliste_Bunker_Bay.rescue_jet_ski_available: Surf Life Saving patrol in season (Sep–Apr) on patrolled beaches
Cape_Naturaliste_Bunker_Bay.accommodation_options: Holiday rentals, caravan parks, hotels nearby
Cape_Naturaliste_Bunker_Bay.drone_allowed: Yes outside no-fly zones; check CASA app
Cape_Naturaliste_Bunker_Bay.annual_competition_window: Local boardrider clubs run year-round comps
Cape_Naturaliste_Bunker_Bay.community_events: Quiksilver King of the Groms (juniors) occasional
Cape_Naturaliste_Bunker_Bay.name: Bunker Bay / Cape Naturaliste (Chinese: 邦克灣;WA south west)
Cape_Naturaliste_Bunker_Bay.gps_location: Approx. -33.5400, 115.0290; Dunsborough, WA. Google Maps: 'Bunker Bay Beach'
Cape_Naturaliste_Bunker_Bay.wave_type: Sheltered point break + beach break;protected from raw SW swell by Cape Naturaliste;good crisp waves when MR is closed out
Cape_Naturaliste_Bunker_Bay.skill_level: 全程度 — Beginner-friendly on small days;Intermediate-Advanced on big days
Cape_Naturaliste_Bunker_Bay.best_season: Year-round; sheltered from heaviest winter SW groundswell
